data.narrative:
Planet Labs is a publicly listed Space & Aerospace company founded by Boshuizen, Marshall, and Schingler, currently valued at $7B+ after raising over $700M. The valuation universe comprises 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private comparables in the same sector.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Across 3 same-sector, same-stage public comparables—Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M)—the median valuation is $6.3B. This range spans $900M to $37.0B, reflecting significant dispersion in how public markets value Space & Aerospace operators at comparable maturity levels. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 42.9x across the sector) cannot be applied, as target revenue is not on file—a common limitation for revenue disclosure in this sector.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $5.4B to $7.2B, with a median of $6.3B. Planet Labs' current $7B+ positioning sits at the upper boundary of this range, slightly above peer median. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among public comparables, which suggests material differences in business model, market position, or growth trajectory that a three-company median may not fully capture. The absence of revenue-based validation limits confidence in the range precision.
